title: LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen vs Semantic Kernel — Decision Matrix
concept: Four production frameworks, each optimizing a different axis: LangGraph=control, CrewAI=speed, AutoGen=async scale, Semantic Kernel=enterprise plugins. All now support MCP, reducing lock-in.
why_it_matters: Framework choice determines refactor cost when the twin moves from PoC to daily use. Match framework to workload shape, not hype.
learn_5min: Read LangGraph 'Why LangGraph?' + CrewAI concepts page + one AutoGen v0.4 tutorial.
apply_10min: Run the provided LangGraph ReAct+OKF stub, then port to CrewAI; compare readability vs. control.

reference_projects.name: LangGraph
reference_projects.description: Graph-based orchestration with checkpointing; best for stateful, cyclic agent workflows.

reference_projects.name: CrewAI
reference_projects.description: Role-based multi-agent framework optimized for rapid prototyping.

reference_projects.name: AutoGen
reference_projects.description: Microsoft's async, event-driven multi-agent framework (v0.4+ actor model).

reference_projects.name: Semantic Kernel
reference_projects.description: Enterprise plugin architecture for .NET, Python, and Java.

consulting_angle: Deliver a 1-page framework decision matrix as a client artifact — pick by workload shape, not brand.
twin_platform_hook: LangGraph as outer orchestrator (checkpoints = resumable). Wrap CrewAI/AutoGen inside nodes if a role library helps.

title: Ontology Design for a Human Twin (FOAF + schema.org/Person + custom)
concept: Compose 3 layers: identity/social (FOAF, schema:Person), knowledge/content (schema:CreativeWork, SIOC), persona/behavior (custom twin: namespace). Reuse before invent.
why_it_matters: Ontology is the twin's DNA — right once = clean composition forever; wrong = refactor pain scaling with data volume.
learn_5min: Skim schema.org/Person + FOAF Person/knows sections + SIOC intro paragraph.
apply_10min: Build twin_ontology_v0.jsonld from the provided template, validate at json-ld.org/playground, then add one twin:Belief with evidence.

reference_projects.name: FOAF vocabulary
reference_projects.description: Canonical Friend-of-a-Friend ontology for people and social relations.

reference_projects.name: schema.org
reference_projects.description: Web-scale vocabulary; the Person class is the pragmatic default.

reference_projects.name: SIOC
reference_projects.description: Semantically-Interlinked Online Communities — posts, threads, discussions.

reference_projects.name: Cognitive Atlas
reference_projects.description: Reference ontology for cognitive concepts — useful if modeling reasoning styles.

consulting_angle: Sell 'AI-ready knowledge modeling' workshops as billable deliverables — direct line to LLM ROI narrative.
twin_platform_hook: Version ontology in GitHub (ontology/twin.ttl + twin.jsonld); every schema change is a PR; SHACL-lint via GitHub Actions.

title: Cloud-Neutral Vector + Graph Reference Stack
concept: Four storage roles (object, graph, vector, keyword) — pick per-layer across Azure/AWS/GCP/OSS. Graph is canonical; vector and BM25 are derived, rebuildable indexes.
why_it_matters: Cloud-neutral design preserves negotiation leverage and lets the twin ship as a portable platform, not a vendor-locked artifact.
learn_5min: Read Neo4j Vector Search docs + Qdrant Quick Start + one hybrid-search tutorial from any cloud.
apply_10min: Run the provided docker-compose (Neo4j+Qdrant+MinIO) locally and execute the Python round-trip to persist a node, embed, upsert, and resolve.

reference_projects.name: Neo4j GenAI Stack
reference_projects.description: Docker starter combining Neo4j + LLM + vector search.

reference_projects.name: Qdrant
reference_projects.description: Open-source vector DB with hybrid search and payload filtering.

reference_projects.name: Apache Jena Fuseki
reference_projects.description: RDF triplestore with SPARQL endpoint — pure W3C route.

reference_projects.name: pgvector
reference_projects.description: Vector search in Postgres; zero-ops for small twins.

consulting_angle: Sell 'cloud-neutral, per-layer choice' as CIO-language architecture — reframes AI as portable infrastructure.
twin_platform_hook: Local dev = docker-compose (Neo4j+Qdrant+MinIO). Prod = swap each layer to managed equivalent with zero code change.

title: Tool Use & Function Calling — Patterns That Survive Production
concept: Five production patterns: narrow contracts, idempotency keys, structured errors with hints, progressive disclosure, MCP-first. Fixes 80% of agent reliability issues.
why_it_matters: Most 'unreliable agent' complaints are sloppy tool contracts, not bad models. Contract quality = agent quality.
learn_5min: Read OpenAI Function calling guide + Anthropic Tool use overview; note the schema convergence.
apply_10min: Implement the okf_query tool from the code snippet; call it via both OpenAI and Anthropic; deliberately break it to observe hint-driven self-correction.

reference_projects.name: OpenAI Cookbook — Function calling
reference_projects.description: Canonical function-calling patterns from OpenAI engineering.

reference_projects.name: Anthropic Cookbook — Tool use
reference_projects.description: Tool-use recipes including hint-driven correction and parallel calls.

reference_projects.name: modelcontextprotocol/servers
reference_projects.description: Reference MCP tool implementations across many domains.

consulting_angle: Publish a 'Tool Contract Standard' as a client artifact; sell the standard and audits against it.
twin_platform_hook: Every OKF operation = one narrow MCP tool; supervisor routes, specialists execute.

title: PROV-O in Practice — Making Every Twin Answer Auditable
concept: PROV-O tracks Entity (what), Activity (how), Agent (who) plus core relations (wasGeneratedBy, used, wasAttributedTo, wasDerivedFrom). Covers 80% of provenance needs.
why_it_matters: Without provenance, the twin is a black box. With PROV-O, every answer is auditable — the line between toy and enterprise IP.
learn_5min: Read PROV-O Primer sections 1–3 (concepts + core diagram). Skip the OWL formalism first pass.
apply_10min: Run the wrap_with_prov() Python snippet on one extracted fact; verify RDF triple count via rdflib/pyld.

reference_projects.name: PROV-O W3C Primer
reference_projects.description: Canonical W3C introduction to provenance modeling.

reference_projects.name: rdflib + pyld
reference_projects.description: Python toolchain for RDF and JSON-LD manipulation.

reference_projects.name: neosemantics (n10s)
reference_projects.description: Neo4j plugin to ingest RDF/JSON-LD directly into property graph.

consulting_angle: In regulated industries, AI auditability is a board-level concern. PROV-O is the answer clients don't yet know to ask for.
twin_platform_hook: Wrap every ingestion output with wrap_with_prov(); store as :Provenance subgraph in Neo4j; Reasoning Agent cites sources by default.
